text.title: Laravel Queues Anti-Patterns
text.summary: Full model in payload, missing failed(), swallowed exceptions, Horizon balancing defaults, database driver at scale.

antipattern.description: Passing a full Eloquent model as a job constructor argument instead of its ID.
antipattern.reason: SerializesModels re-fetches the model on handle(); if it was soft-deleted or updated, the deserialized model is stale or throws ModelNotFoundException. For large tables, the serialized payload also grows unnecessarily.

antipattern.description: Missing failed() and retryUntil() on production jobs that mutate state.
antipattern.reason: Without failed(), terminal failures are silent — the resource stays in a pending state indefinitely. Without retryUntil(), time-sensitive jobs retry long after the retry window is meaningful.

antipattern.description: try/catch inside handle() swallowing exceptions to "be safe" — defeats the retry mechanism.
antipattern.reason: Laravel retries jobs when handle() throws an exception. Swallowing exceptions makes handle() always appear to succeed, so retries never fire and the job silently completes in a broken state.

antipattern.description: Using the database queue driver at high volume (>50 jobs/s) without switching to Redis + Horizon.
antipattern.reason: The database driver uses row locking on the jobs table; at high throughput, workers contend on locks, degrading to serial processing. Redis with Horizon is non-blocking and orders of magnitude faster.

antipattern.description: Horizon balancing strategy left at the default (simple) without configuring min/max per queue.
antipattern.reason: Horizon auto-balancing with simple strategy allocates workers evenly regardless of queue depth; low-traffic queues get the same share as high-traffic ones, starving critical queues under load.

antipattern.description: Deploying without restarting queue workers via php artisan queue:restart.
antipattern.reason: Queue workers are long-running processes that loaded the old code at startup; without a restart, they continue executing old business logic while the web layer runs new code.
